seoTitle: Add a PDF File to Webflow
metaDescription: How to upload a PDF into Webflow's asset manager and link it to a button or text element for visitors to download.
intro: Offering a downloadable catalog, portfolio, or e-book doesn't require an external file-hosting service — Webflow's asset manager can host PDFs directly and serve them through its own CDN. This guide covers uploading a document and linking it to a clickable button or text element.

steps.heading: Open the Assets panel
steps.body: Click the Assets folder icon in the left-hand sidebar to reach your media library.

steps.heading: Upload the PDF
steps.body: Click the cloud upload icon at the top of the tray and select your local PDF file to import it.

steps.heading: Copy the hosted link
steps.body: Hover over the newly uploaded file, click its Gear icon, and copy the unique CDN URL.

steps.heading: Bind it to a button
steps.body: Select a button on your canvas, open Element Settings, and use the File attachment option with the paperclip icon to map the PDF directly.

steps.heading: Or link it to text or a link block
steps.body: Alternatively, select a link block or text string, switch the link type to URL, paste the copied path, and enable Open in New Tab so it doesn't overwrite your page.

faq.question: Where does Webflow store the uploaded PDF?
faq.answer: It's hosted on Webflow's own CDN and served through a unique link generated when you upload it to the asset manager.

faq.question: Should downloads open in a new tab?
faq.answer: Yes, enabling Open in New Tab keeps visitors from losing their place on your main page when they click a document link.

faq.question: Can I attach a PDF directly to a button instead of a text link?
faq.answer: Yes, the Element Settings for a button include a File attachment option that maps a hosted document straight to that element.

faq.question: How do I check the download actually works before publishing?
faq.answer: Use the Live Site Preview mode and click your download links directly to confirm the behavior before pushing changes live.
